lines.en_lit: It is a wooden vessel designated to rest Any wooden vessel designated to rest is not susceptible to ritual impurity, and it serves as a barrier before impurity, Rather, teaches lift it to the Festival pilgrims, and they say to them: See how beloved you are before the Omnipresent, as its removal as its arrangement, as it is stated: “To put out hot bread on the day it was taken away”

And are there no more But didn’t Rav Oshaya say: When Solomon built the Temple he planted all sorts of precious golden fruits there, and brought forth their fruit in their season and when the wind blew them would fall, as it is stated: “May his fruits rustle like Lebanon” And when the gentiles entered the Sanctuary withered, as it stated: “And the blossoms of Lebanon wither” And the Holy One, Blessed be He, will restore the in the future, as it is stated: “It shall blossom abundantly, it shall also rejoice and shout, the glory of Lebanon will be given to it”
does not count perpetual miracles Now that you have arrived at this The Ark and the cherubs they too were perpetual miracles.

The Master said the smoke of the arrangement of wood. And did smoke the arrangement of wood But wasn’t it taught five matters stated with regard to the fire of the arrangement of wood: It crouched like a lion; and it was clear sun; and it had substance to consume wet like dry and it did not raise smoke.
When we said person, as it was taught “And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar” even though fire descends from the heavens, mitzva to bring fire by a person.
crouched like a lion? But wasn’t it taught Rabbi Ḥanina, the deputy Priest, said: I saw crouched like a dog not difficult. Here, First Temple; there, Second Temple.

And was there in the Second Temple? Didn’t Rav Shmuel bar Inya say: What is which is written and I will look on it favorably and I will be glorified [ve’ekkaved], we read it ve’ikkavda, What is different missing heh? This five, phenomena between First Temple Second Temple, And these are: The Ark and the Ark cover and the cherubs fire; and Divine Presence; and the Divine Spirit; and the Urim VeTummim. say Yes, there was it did not assist

The Sages taught there are six kinds of fire: There is consumes solids and does not consume liquids; and there is consumes liquids and does not consume solids; and there is consumes solids and consumes liquids; and there is consumes wet like dry and there is fire that repels fire; and there is fire that consumes fire.

There is fire that consumes solids and does not consume liquids; that is our consumes liquids and does not consume solids of the sick consumes solids and consumes liquids of Elijah as it is written: and it licked up the water that was in the trench” consumes wet like dry of the arrangement of wood. There is fire that repels fire; of Gabriel. And there is fire that consumes fire; of the Divine Presence, as the Master said extended His finger and burned them.

And the smoke of the arrangement, even if all the winds in the world come and blow it, they do not move it from its place Didn’t Rabbi Yitzḥak bar Avdimi say: At the conclusion of the final day of the festival everyone looks to the smoke of the arrangement of wood; tends toward the north, poor were glad and the homeowners were sad. because that the year’s rains would be plentiful, but the fruit would rot tend toward the south, the poor were sad and the homeowners were glad, because that the year’s rains would be sparse. to store the fruit
to the east, everyone was glad. to the west and everyone was sad. comes and goes like a palm tree, but it did not disperse.

The Master said: to the east everyone is glad; to the west everyone was sad. And raised a contradiction, An eastern is always good; a western is always bad; a northern is good for wheat when reached one-third and bad for olives when they are ripening; a southern is bad for wheat when reached one-third and good for olives when they are ripening.
And Rav Yosef said, and some say it was Mar Zutra And your mnemonic table was in the north and the candelabrum was in the south this increased its own and this increased its own
not difficult: This is for us, This is for them,
